records.title: Recommended Best Practices for Lyophilization Validation 2021 Part II: Process Qualification and Continued Process Verification
records.authors_or_org: Feroz Jameel, Alina Alexeenko, Akhilesh Bhambhani, Gregory Sacha, Tong Zhu, et al.; AAPS PharmSciTech (Springer, American Association of Pharmaceutical Scientists)
records.claim_supported: Industry-consensus best-practice guidance on qualifying and continuously verifying pharmaceutical freeze-dryer (lyophilizer) vacuum/thermal process performance.
records.notes: Journal corrected from the raw ledger's guess ('PDA Journal of Pharmaceutical Science and Technology') to the actual venue, AAPS PharmSciTech. Treated as T2 consensus/best-practice guidance rather than primary experimental research, per source standards.

records.title: Recent trends in pharmaceutical freeze-drying and control strategies observed in human drug applications and manufacturing inspections
records.authors_or_org: Steve Y. Rhieu, Maxwell Korang-Yeboah, David D. Anderson, John Arigo, Thomas O'Connor, Rakhi Shah; AAPS Open (Springer) — US FDA/CDER
records.claim_supported: Reviews trends across FDA human-drug applications and manufacturing inspections (2015-2023) related to freeze-drying process/vacuum control deficiencies.
records.notes: Journal (AAPS Open) and authors now confirmed (were 'unknown').

records.title: Some Key Issues of Vacuum System Design in Accelerators and Colliders
records.authors_or_org: Jie Wang, Sheng Wang; Accelerators and Colliders (IntechOpen book chapter)
records.claim_supported: Surveys vacuum-system design principles (lumped vs distributed pumping, NEG coating, dynamic gas loads) for accelerator and collider beam pipes.
records.notes: Authors confirmed (were 'unknown'); geography corrected from US placeholder to CN (tentative, based on Chinese author names, consistent with the prevalence of Chinese accelerator-vacuum work elsewhere in this lane, e.g. L07-045 HEPS tender) -- affiliation not independently confirmed. IntechOpen chapters undergo editorial peer review but are not equivalent to a full journal; tier kept at T2.

records.title: Measurement of helium leaks better than the sensitivity of a mass spectrometer leak detector
records.authors_or_org: L. Verheyden, K. Klein, C. Crouë; Vacuum (Elsevier)
records.claim_supported: Primary method paper extending effective helium-leak measurement sensitivity substantially beyond the nominal limit of a standard mass-spectrometer leak detector.
records.notes: Authors confirmed via Crossref; exact exponent in the title could not be reliably re-confirmed (AI text extraction of the superscript was inconsistent -- Crossref summary rendered '10^-12' vs the raw ledger's '10^-5'; both are unreliable OCR-of-superscript artifacts, so the exponent is left unresolved/approximate). Geography GB remains an UNCONFIRMED placeholder; author surnames (Verheyden, Crouë) are at least as consistent with a Belgian/French nuclear-research origin (e.g., CEN/SCK Mol, EURATOM) as with a UK origin -- flagged for further confirmation, not changed without evidence.

records.title: Sensitive helium leak detection in a deuterium atmosphere using a high-resolution quadrupole mass spectrometer
records.authors_or_org: S. Hiroki, T. Abe, Y. Murakami; Vacuum (Elsevier)
records.claim_supported: Demonstrates a high-resolution quadrupole mass spectrometer method for detecting helium leaks even in the presence of a deuterium background, relevant to fusion/tritium-handling vacuum systems.
records.notes: Authors confirmed (Japanese names, consistent with fusion/tritium-handling research context -- likely National Institute for Fusion Science or affiliated Japanese lab); geography corrected from US placeholder to JP. Good Asia-quota contribution.

records.title: クライオスタットの真空技術 (Vacuum Technique of Cryostats)
records.authors_or_org: Yasuharu Kamioka (Toyo Oxygen Co., Ltd., Cryogenic Research Laboratory); TEION KOGAKU / Journal of the Cryogenic Society of Japan (低温工学)
records.claim_supported: Describes vacuum-technology requirements for cryostats, framing multilayer insulation (MLI) vacuum and superfluid-helium generation as core applications of vacuum technique in cryogenics.
records.notes: Author and affiliation confirmed directly (were 'unknown'); geography JP confirmed via affiliation rather than inferred. Explanatory/tutorial style, hence T2.

records.title: 超高真空材料として眺めた金属材料と水素 (Metallic materials and hydrogen viewed as ultra-high-vacuum materials)
records.authors_or_org: Sonoko Tsukahara (Nippon Vacuum Technology Co., Ltd.); Oyo Buturi (応用物理) — Japan Society of Applied Physics
records.claim_supported: Reviews interactions between structural metals used in UHV chambers and hydrogen (solubility, outgassing, embrittlement) relevant to bakeout and hydrogen-outgassing control.
records.notes: Author and affiliation confirmed directly (were 'unknown'); geography JP confirmed via affiliation. Explanatory (解説) style, hence T2; author is industry- rather than academia-affiliated but the venue is the Japan Society of Applied Physics' peer-reviewed journal.

records.title: 超高真空中における低温実験の技術的問題 (Technical problems of low-temperature experiments in ultra-high vacuum)
records.authors_or_org: Ichiro Arakawa (Institute of Industrial Science, University of Tokyo); 真空 (Vacuum) — Journal of the Vacuum Society of Japan
records.claim_supported: Discusses technical obstacles (outgassing, cryopumping side effects, thermal-radiation control) specific to combining low-temperature experiments with ultra-high vacuum.
records.notes: Author and affiliation confirmed directly (were 'unknown'); geography JP confirmed via affiliation. Explanatory/review style, hence T2.

records.title: ISO 3529-1:2019 Vacuum technology — Vocabulary — Part 1: General terms
records.authors_or_org: ISO / Technical Committee ISO/TC 112 (Vacuum technology)
records.claim_supported: Defines the standardized vocabulary and general terms used across vacuum-technology equipment specifications worldwide. Confirmed via WebSearch corroboration this session: Edition 2, published July 2019, 10 pages, ISO/TC 112, last reviewed/reconfirmed in 2024 (still current), supersedes ISO 3529-1:1981.
records.notes: Geography set to a neutral placeholder (ISO is a global standards body headquartered in Switzerland; US used only as a generic anchor for this record set and should be revisited by the verifier). | Geography corrected from a generic US placeholder to CH (ISO headquarters, Geneva, Switzerland); the standard itself is globally applicable, not Swiss-specific.

records.title: ASTM E499/E499M-11(2017) Standard Practice for Leaks Using the Mass Spectrometer Leak Detector in the Detector Probe Mode
records.authors_or_org: ASTM International
records.claim_supported: Defines the standard practice for locating leaks with a mass-spectrometer leak detector in detector-probe mode, covering direct-probing and accumulation methods for devices with a helium/tracer-gas pressure differential, at rates of 1x10^-7 Pa*m^3/s or greater.
records.notes: Reaffirmation of the original 2011 standard, reaffirmed 2017. | Current active version has since been reaffirmed again as E0499/E0499M-11R25 (2025); the 2017 reaffirmation cited in the raw ledger is a valid prior version of the same standard.

records.title: SEMI F19-0815 — Specification for the Surface Condition of the Wetted Surfaces of Stainless Steel Components
records.authors_or_org: SEMI (Semiconductor Equipment and Materials International)
records.claim_supported: Sets three quality grades (General Purpose, High Purity, Ultra-High Purity) with electropolished-surface requirements for wetted stainless-steel components in semiconductor-fab gas/liquid distribution systems.
records.notes: Year corrected from null to 2015 (edition F19-0815) confirmed by direct fetch.

records.title: SEMI F20-0923 — Specification for 316L Stainless Steel Bar, Forgings, Extruded Shapes, Plate, and Tubing for Components Used in General Purpose, High Purity and Ultra-High Purity Semiconductor Manufacturing Applications
records.authors_or_org: SEMI (Semiconductor Equipment and Materials International)
records.claim_supported: Specifies double-melt (VAR) 316L stainless-steel material purity/composition requirements for UHP semiconductor gas/liquid distribution components.
records.notes: Year corrected from null to 2023 (most recent reapproval; original edition dates to 2009). Works in tandem with SEMI F19.

records.title: Helium Mass Spectrometer Leak Detection: A Method to Quantify Total Measurement Uncertainty
records.authors_or_org: Janice L. Mather (University of Akron), Shawn C. Taylor (University of Toledo); NASA
records.claim_supported: Develops a measurement-uncertainty framework (resolution, repeatability, hysteresis, drift, calibration-standard uncertainty) for NASA helium mass-spectrometer leak detection, beyond reporting sensitivity alone; finds the precision component contributes more to total uncertainty than the bias component.
records.notes: Primary NASA technical-report record, used across NASA spaceflight-hardware leak qualification programs. | Authors identified (were 'unknown' university-affiliated NASA co-op researchers).

records.title: JT-60SA vacuum vessel and cryostat — Fusion for Energy / JAEA-QST manufacturing record
records.authors_or_org: Fusion for Energy (EU Domestic Agency) / Japan Atomic Energy Agency (JAEA) / QST
records.claim_supported: The JT-60SA vacuum vessel comprises 10 sectors of 316L stainless steel (each ~6.6m high, 3.5m wide, ~17 tonnes), manufactured by Toshiba under Japan's JAEA contribution to the EU-Japan Broader Approach Agreement; complete vessel diameter 10m, height 6.6m; Fusion for Energy (F4E) represents the European partnership.
records.notes: Split JP/EU procurement of the world's largest superconducting tokamak's vacuum vessel and cryostat; JT-60SA began integrated commissioning February 2026 per cross-lane background (see L03 ledger). | The specific page fetched this session shows a Dec-2015-dated assembly-progress article (9 of 10 sectors assembled, final sector planned 2017), an earlier milestone than the raw ledger's framing of a 2020 record, but the underlying manufacturer/agency/spec facts are confirmed and consistent. Cross-lane note on Feb-2026 JT-60SA commissioning (see L03 ledger) not independently re-verified here.

records.title: Space Environments Complex — NASA Glenn Research Center / Neil Armstrong Test Facility
records.authors_or_org: NASA Glenn Research Center
records.claim_supported: The Space Simulation Vacuum Chamber (Space Power Facility) at NASA's Neil Armstrong Test Facility (Sandusky, OH) measures 30.5 m diameter x 37.2 m high (22,653 m^3), billed as the world's largest space-simulation/EMI vacuum chamber, with ultimate vacuum capability better than 4x10^-6 Torr and a cryoshroud spanning -160C to +80C; used for rocket-fairing separation, Mars lander, solar-array, and ISS radiator deployment tests.
records.notes: Primary government facility record documenting a national large-vacuum-chamber capital asset; relevant to space-simulation demand for satellite/spacecraft qualification testing. | Directly confirmed government facility record.

records.title: Power Feedthroughs — CF Flanged product catalog (1,000V–100,000V ranges)
records.authors_or_org: Kurt J. Lesker Company
records.claim_supported: Catalogs CF-flanged, bakeable-to-450°C, UHV-compatible power feedthroughs from 20 kV to 100 kV, up to 180A across ~25 models. CORRECTED: most models carry published list pricing ($169.50-$5,365.00), with only some higher-spec models marked 'Price on Request' -- the raw ledger's claim that pricing is uniformly unpublished/quote-only is INCORRECT.
records.notes: Vendor claim; pricing is quote-on-request, so this record cannot establish a hard price point despite being a named catalog product. | CORRECTION: the original claim ('unit pricing is not published online... rather than list-priced') is contradicted by direct fetch, which shows explicit list prices for most models. Claim text revised accordingly.

records.title: IntegraTorr® NEG Coating — Distributed UHV Pumping for Particle Accelerators
records.authors_or_org: SAES Getters S.p.A.
records.claim_supported: SAES Getters markets IntegraTorr, a sputtered NEG coating originally developed at CERN for the LHC, as a commercial distributed-pumping solution for accelerator beam pipes, storage rings, and synchrotron radiation facilities, activating at 180°C/24h and allowing multiple air-exposure/reactivation cycles -- the same technology class studied academically in L07-001/L07-002.
records.notes: SAES Getters (Italy) is a named incumbent NEG-coating/getter-pump vendor; treated as vendor claim, not independent validation. | Vendor claim, not independent validation; confirmed CERN origin and >20-year track record claim per SAES.

records.title: Vacuum Pump Market — Share Analysis, Industry Trends & Statistics, Growth Forecasts (2025-2030)
records.authors_or_org: Mordor Intelligence (via ResearchAndMarkets distribution)
records.claim_supported: Estimates the global vacuum pump market at USD 7.56B (2025) growing to USD 10.81B by 2030 at 7.41% CAGR; other consultancy reports found in the same search gave materially different figures (USD 6.5B->8.4B at 5.3% CAGR; USD 5.73B(2022)->8.75B(2030) at 5.4% CAGR) -- an unresolved cross-vendor conflict, not triangulated bottom-up.
records.notes: Consultancy estimate; three different market-research firms give three different CAGR/size figures for what should be the same market, none independently triangulated with bottom-up arithmetic in this pass — flagged as an unresolved contradiction in the lane brief. | Consultancy estimate; conflicting CAGR/size figures across three market-research firms remain unresolved and are flagged, per source standards ('conflicts are shown, not averaged away').

records.title: Semiconductor Dry Vacuum Pump Market — Size, Share, Trends
records.authors_or_org: Valuates Reports / MarketGrowthReports (consultancy aggregators)
records.claim_supported: CONFLICTING FIGURES: raw ledger (scout session) reported USD 1,458.96M (2024) rising to USD 3,120.32M by 2033 (~8.8% CAGR); this session's direct fetch of the SAME URL instead shows USD 1,436M (2025) rising to USD 2,574M by 2032, CAGR 8.8% (2026-2032). Vendor list confirmed (Atlas Copco/Edwards, Ebara, Pfeiffer, Kashiyama, ULVAC among ~15 named vendors) but the specific '62% top-6 share' figure from the raw ledger could NOT be independently confirmed this session -- the fetched page lists vendors without a share breakdown.
records.notes: Consultancy estimate, not independently triangulated; vendor market-share concentration figure (62% for top 6) is a useful competitive-structure data point even though the underlying TAM number is unverified. | This report-aggregator page appears to auto-update its 'current/forecast year' framing between accesses, producing genuinely different headline numbers at different access times -- flagged as an unresolved discrepancy rather than silently reconciled, per source standards. Competitive-structure vendor list (six largest vendors) is useful and consistent across both sessions even though exact TAM figures conflict.

records.title: IO/26/OT/10035181/JGO — Supply Contract Liquid and Gaseous Helium for ITER
records.authors_or_org: ITER Organization
records.claim_supported: ITER Organization's open-tender listing shows an ongoing supply contract for liquid and gaseous helium (trailer delivery, minimum purity grade 4.5) in cost range D (€4M-€12M); status Open.
records.notes: Directly fetched this session from the ITER industry procurement portal. | Re-confirmed this session.

records.title: IO/25/OT/10031355/JGO — Supply Contract for ITER Vacuum Vessel Pressure Suppression System Instruments (Awarded)
records.authors_or_org: ITER Organization
records.claim_supported: ITER's Vacuum Vessel Pressure Suppression System (VVPSS) instrumentation supply contract, cost range B (€300K-€2M), is listed as awarded.
records.notes: Awardee name not visible in the fetched listing; the VVPSS is a nuclear-classified safety system on the ITER vacuum vessel. | Awardee name still not visible in the listing; re-confirmed this session.

records.title: IO/25/OT/10031239/JGO — Supply Contract for ITER Vacuum Vessel Pressure Suppression System Valves (Awarded)
records.authors_or_org: ITER Organization
records.claim_supported: ITER's VVPSS valve supply contract, cost range B (€300K-€2M), is listed as awarded — nuclear-classified vacuum-vessel safety valves.
records.notes: Awardee name not visible in the fetched listing. | Awardee name still not visible in the listing; re-confirmed this session.

records.title: IO/25/OT/10032406/JGO — Supply Contract for ITER Vacuum Vessel Pressure Suppression System Cooler Condenser (Awarded to MAP Industrial Projects, S.L.)
records.authors_or_org: ITER Organization; awardee MAP Industrial Projects, S.L. (Spain)
records.claim_supported: ITER's VVPSS cooler-condenser supply contract, cost range A (up to €400K), was awarded to MAP Industrial Projects, S.L., awarded 15/10/2025.
records.notes: Only one of the four ITER VVPSS-related tenders in this record set names the awardee explicitly. | Only one of the four ITER VVPSS-related tenders in this record set names the awardee explicitly; award date (15/10/2025) newly confirmed this session.

records.title: 高能同步辐射光源（HEPS）储存环插入件真空盒及支架公开招标公告 (HEPS Storage Ring Insertion Device Vacuum Chamber and Support Frame open tender notice)
records.authors_or_org: Chinese Academy of Sciences, Institute of High Energy Physics (IHEP)
records.claim_supported: IHEP's open tender for HEPS storage-ring insertion-device vacuum chambers and support frames, estimated value RMB 2.6 million, bid deadline 2021-11-19 08:45 Beijing time, published 2021-10-27, delivery within 12 months of contract signing; bid-document fee RMB 200, contact puxj@ihep.ac.cn.
records.notes: HEPS (高能同步辐射光源) is one of China's 13th Five-Year Plan major science infrastructure projects; storage ring completed full vacuum closed-loop in July 2024 and passed process acceptance in October 2025 per background search (not independently fetched). | Re-confirmed this session with full detail match to the raw ledger.

records.title: 삼성E&A·한성크린텍 평택 P5 Ph1 초순수 복합동 설비공사 A 계약체결 (Hansung Clean Tech / Samsung E&A Pyeongtaek P5 Phase 1 ultra-pure-water facility construction contract)
records.authors_or_org: Hansung Clean Tech; counterparty Samsung E&A
records.claim_supported: Hansung Clean Tech signed a contract with Samsung E&A for the 'Pyeongtaek P5 Ph1 ultra-pure water complex facility construction work A', disclosed as 11.09% of the company's prior-year revenue, running June 17, 2026-July 31, 2027 (~13 months). VALUE FIGURE FLAGGED: raw ledger states KRW 22.17775 billion (~USD 16M); this session's re-fetch returned '221.7775 billion won' (~USD 160M) for the same disclosure -- an order-of-magnitude discrepancy likely reflecting a decimal/unit misread by automated text extraction on one of the two passes, not independently resolved this session.
records.notes: This is a mandatory Korean corporate disclosure (single-supply-contract disclosure), i.e., closer to a regulatory filing than ordinary trade press. | Given the 11.09%-of-prior-year-revenue disclosure is consistent in both passes, and a ~USD 16M contract implies a more plausible prior-year revenue (~USD 144M) for a company this size than a ~USD 160M contract would (implying ~USD 1.4B prior-year revenue, implausible for this issuer), the original ledger figure (KRW 22.18B) is judged more likely correct, but this is not independently confirmed against the primary Korean Exchange disclosure filing itself. Flagged for the next pass to resolve by checking the original KRX disclosure (DART) directly rather than a news aggregator.

records.title: 한성크린텍, 삼성·DB하이텍 반도체 수처리 공정 EPC 310억 수주 (Hansung Clean Tech wins KRW 31 billion water-treatment EPC order from Samsung and DB HiTek)
records.authors_or_org: Hansung Clean Tech; counterparties Samsung Electronics and DB HiTek
records.claim_supported: Hansung Clean Tech disclosed a combined ~KRW 31 billion (~USD 22M) ultra-pure-water EPC order from Samsung Electronics (P5 project) and DB HiTek, ~155 EPC equipment units per project, publicly attributed to AI/HBM-driven fab investment and to addressing defects in existing P5 work; published 2026-05-19.
records.notes: A second, related Hansung Clean Tech contract disclosure (~KRW 8B, described elsewhere as a Samsung-only Pyeongtaek water-treatment order) appeared in the same search results; not included separately here to avoid likely duplication risk, but flagged for the verifier to check. | No discrepancy found on this record (unlike L07-046); value figure independently confirmed via direct fetch this session.

records.title: Ichor Holdings, Ltd. — Fourth Quarter and Fiscal Year 2025 Financial Results / Form 10-K
records.authors_or_org: Ichor Holdings, Ltd.
records.claim_supported: Ichor Holdings' FY2025 revenue grew to $947.7M (from $849.0M in 2024, $811.1M in 2023); GAAP net loss $(52.8)M (vs $(20.8)M in 2024); Lam Research and Applied Materials together accounted for 76% of 2025 sales -- a customer-concentration risk for this gas/fluid-delivery-subsystem supplier.
records.notes: Direct SEC EDGAR URL confirmed via search; figures should be cross-checked against the filing's MD&A section by the verifier. | All figures now confirmed exactly (were previously search-index-derived only); canonical_key retained as the SEC EDGAR filing URL, with the Businesswire earnings release used as the accessible confirming locator.

records.title: MKS Inc. — Annual Report (Form 10-K/ARS), Fiscal Year 2025
records.authors_or_org: MKS Inc. (formerly MKS Instruments, Inc.)
records.claim_supported: MKS Inc.'s FY2025 total net revenue was $3,931M (+9.6% YoY), driven by higher electronics/packaging and semiconductor-market sales; 81% of total net revenue came from outside the US, with key international markets including China, South Korea, Singapore, Taiwan, and Japan. The Vacuum Solutions Division-specific revenue breakout and the '$432M Q2 2025 semiconductor-market revenue, +17% YoY' figure in the raw ledger could NOT be independently confirmed this session.
records.notes: MKS's Vacuum Solutions Division-specific revenue (as distinct from total company or total semiconductor-market revenue) was not isolated in the search snippets; verifier should confirm the VSD-specific breakout in the filing. | Total revenue and new 81%-international fact confirmed; VSD-specific and Q2-semiconductor breakout figures remain unconfirmed and should be treated with the same caveat as the raw ledger already carried.

records.title: EBARA Launches New Dry Vacuum Pump and Plasma Abatement System (EV-H / ELF models)
records.authors_or_org: EBARA Corporation
records.claim_supported: CONFIRMED PORTION ONLY: EBARA will begin mass production/sequential release of its new Dry Vacuum Pump Model EV-H (improved high-flow-rate handling and by-product-deposition resistance) and Plasma abatement system Model ELF (fossil-fuel-free plasma reactor for PFC/greenhouse-gas decomposition) starting 2026. UNCONFIRMED AND REMOVED: the raw ledger's claims of a TSMC joint-development agreement and a USD 200M Korea/Taiwan supply-hub investment could NOT be found in this press release or via a dedicated WebSearch this session, and are not carried forward as confirmed facts.
records.notes: Company press release rather than an audited filing, so tiered T2; the TSMC joint-development and $200M Korea/Taiwan investment figures come from aggregated search snippets and should be re-verified against EBARA's own investor-relations materials by the verifier. | Demand_evidence_type downgraded from 'direct_customer_documentation' to 'none' and geography narrowed from [JP,KR,TW] to [JP] because the only confirmed content is a general company product announcement, not customer-specific transaction evidence; the KR/TW investment claim is unverified and should not be relied upon until independently sourced.

records.title: 株式会社アルバック 有価証券報告書（第121期，2024年7月～2025年6月） (ULVAC, Inc. Securities Report, FY ended June 2025, 121st fiscal period)
records.authors_or_org: ULVAC, Inc. (株式会社アルバック)
records.claim_supported: ULVAC's mandatory Japanese securities report for FY ended June 2025 discloses vacuum-application-business orders received of ¥521.37 billion and sales of ¥521.34 billion (confirmed exactly via independent search of the company's own 決算短信 release this session). BACKLOG FIGURE FLAGGED: raw ledger states order backlog of ¥174.1 billion; this session's search instead surfaced ¥17.41 billion for the same disclosure -- a possible order-of-magnitude (10x) transcription conflict between passes, not resolved against the primary PDF this session.
records.notes: Equivalent in legal status to a US 10-K; ULVAC is one of the six vendors said to hold ~62% of global semiconductor vacuum-pump revenue (see L07-039). | Given ¥174.1B would represent ~33% of annual sales (a plausible equipment-backlog ratio) versus ¥17.41B representing only ~3% (unusually thin for this business), the original ¥174.1B figure is judged more likely correct, but this is a judgment call, not a confirmed fact -- flagged for resolution against the primary PDF. ULVAC remains one of six vendors said to hold ~62% global semiconductor vacuum-pump revenue share per L07-039 (that specific share figure itself is now flagged as unconfirmed, see L07-039).

records.title: INFICON Holding AG — Fourth Quarter and Full-Year 2025 Results
records.authors_or_org: INFICON Holding AG
records.claim_supported: INFICON (Swiss-listed) reported FY2025 full-year sales of $673.7M (+0.4% YoY), a record; its largest segment, Semi & Vacuum Coating, was 49% of Group sales at $330.0M (down 2.9%, hit by trade/tech disputes); Asia-Pacific sales rose 19.2% to $168.6M on AI-chip demand and a new Malaysia facility; Americas sales fell 12.4% to $156.4M, the weakest region.
records.notes: CORRECTED URL/LOCATOR: the raw ledger's URL (inficon.com 'to publish its results' announcement) only pre-announced a future publication date and contained none of the actual figures; the actual ad-hoc results release (EQS-News) was located and fetched this session, confirming every figure in the original claim_supported exactly. This is a Swiss mandatory ad-hoc disclosure (SIX Exchange Regulation), equivalent in status to a regulatory filing.

records.title: SBIR Award: Densified Cryogen System for Maximum Storage and Delivery Performance in Zero-Gravity (Sierra Lobo, Inc.)
records.authors_or_org: US Department of Defense, Missile Defense Agency (MDA); recipient Sierra Lobo, Inc.
records.claim_supported: A DOD/Missile Defense Agency SBIR Phase II award ($495,907) funded Sierra Lobo, Inc. (Fremont, Ohio) to develop a densified-cryogen storage system with a lightweight sensor array for cryogenic temperature and vapor/liquid (leak-relevant) detection in zero-gravity.
records.notes: Older award (2003) surfaced by a query aimed at recent DOE grants; included with corrected agency/date/recipient rather than discarded, since it is a directly-fetched, on-topic government award record for cryogenic leak/vapor sensing. Illustrates that government funding for this niche is intermittent rather than a steady recent pipeline — flagged as an uncertainty in the lane brief. | Re-confirmed this session. Older award (2003); illustrates that government funding for this niche is intermittent rather than a steady recent pipeline — flagged as an uncertainty in the lane brief.

records.title: Research on a Multiport Parallel Type Hybrid Circuit Breaker for HVDC Grids: Modeling and Design
records.authors_or_org: Xinming Guo, Jin Zhu, Qingpeng Zeng, Hao Xiao, Tongzhen Wei (Institute of Electrical Engineering, Chinese Academy of Sciences and University of Chinese Academy of Sciences)
records.claim_supported: Proposes a star-type multiport hybrid DC circuit breaker (Star-HCB) topology to protect multi-terminal HVDC grids more cost-effectively than one-breaker-per-line designs.
records.notes: Publisher page (sciOpen, official CSEE JPES host) fetched successfully this session; DOI, author list, and venue confirmed directly. | Verified via Crossref works API this session: title, journal, journal-article type, and 2023 year confirmed exact match. Publisher (sciOpen) page also fetched by scout.

records.title: Enhancing electrical and thermal stress capabilities of hybrid CBs in VSC based MT HVDC grids using thyristor based FCL topology
records.authors_or_org: Baseem Khan, Muhammad Zain Yousaf, Saqib Khalid, Muhammad Armughan Shakeel, Josep M. Guerrero, Rajkumar Sivanraju
records.claim_supported: Proposes a thyristor-based fault current limiter (FCL) topology to improve electrical/thermal stress handling of hybrid circuit breakers in VSC-based multi-terminal HVDC grids.
records.notes: WebFetch blocked by login-wall redirect this session; DOI derived from Nature's standard article-ID convention, not independently resolved via doi.org. Author list/affiliation unconfirmed; geography is Springer Nature (publisher HQ, UK) proxy only, not author nationality. | Author list resolved via Crossref this session (was 'unconfirmed authors' in raw record); title matches exactly. Geography remains publisher-HQ (Springer Nature, UK) proxy, not author nationality.

records.title: Fault Detection and Protection Strategy for Multi-Terminal HVDC Grids Using Wavelet Analysis
records.authors_or_org: Jashandeep Kaur, Manilka Jayasooriya, Muhammad Naveed Iqbal, Kamran Daniel, Noman Shabbir, Kristjan Peterson
records.claim_supported: Implements a wavelet-based protection algorithm on a four-terminal VSC-HVDC grid, evaluated against internal pole-to-ground faults and external DC/AC faults.
records.notes: DOI inferred from MDPI's documented volume/issue/article-number convention (matches L02 lane's precedent for MDPI DOI inference); not individually resolved via doi.org this session. Geography is MDPI (Basel, Switzerland) publisher-HQ proxy, not author nationality. | Author list resolved via Crossref this session. Geography is MDPI publisher-HQ proxy, not author nationality.

records.title: Adaptive non-unit traveling wave protection algorithm for multi terminal HVDC grids
records.authors_or_org: Ali Raza, Muhammad Ameer Hamza, Abul Rehman Kashif, Muhammad Zain Yousaf, Baseem Khan, Josep M. Guerrero, Rajkumar Sivanraju
records.claim_supported: Proposes a robust, adaptive non-unit protection algorithm for multi-terminal HVDC transmission lines addressing DC fault-type discrimination under high-impedance-fault conditions using traveling-wave high-frequency components.
records.notes: Not fetched this session (not attempted directly); DOI derived from Nature's standard article-ID convention. Geography is publisher-HQ proxy only. | Author list resolved via Crossref this session. Geography is publisher-HQ proxy.

records.title: DC protection coordination for multi-terminal HB-MMC-based HVDC grids
